reason: Avoid or defer MRA when renal impairment is severe or potassium is high.

clinical_sources.title: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA Guideline for the Management of Heart Failure
clinical_sources.note: MRA therapy in HFrEF is recommended only when renal function and serum potassium are acceptable; the rule operationalizes this as a safety stop/review.

reason: MRA may require close potassium and renal monitoring.

clinical_sources.title: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA Guideline for the Management of Heart Failure
clinical_sources.note: The MRA recommendation depends on renal function and potassium, so moderate abnormalities trigger monitoring rather than a simple approve.

reason: RAAS-inhibiting therapy may need caution with low blood pressure or elevated potassium.

clinical_sources.title: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA Guideline for the Management of Heart Failure
clinical_sources.note: RAAS-inhibiting therapies are core HFrEF treatment, but blood pressure, renal function, and potassium influence safe initiation/titration.

clinical_sources.title: KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for CKD
clinical_sources.note: CKD medication management emphasizes concurrent renal function and potassium monitoring for RAS inhibitor use.

reason: Beta blocker initiation or titration requires caution when heart rate is low.

clinical_sources.title: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA Guideline for the Management of Heart Failure
clinical_sources.note: Evidence-based beta blockers are guideline-directed therapy in HFrEF; low heart rate is a patient-specific safety concern for initiation/titration.

reason: SGLT2 inhibitor eligibility and monitoring should be reviewed when renal function is reduced.

clinical_sources.title: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA Guideline for the Management of Heart Failure
clinical_sources.note: SGLT2 inhibitors are part of HFrEF guideline-directed therapy; renal function should be reviewed before drug-specific use.

clinical_sources.title: 2023 ESC Focused Update of Heart Failure Guidelines
clinical_sources.note: The focused update strengthens SGLT2 inhibitor recommendations across heart failure phenotypes.

reason: Medication burden increases interaction, adherence, and sequencing risk.

clinical_sources.title: KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for CKD
clinical_sources.note: Medication management in CKD highlights the need to consider medication burden, monitoring, and safety.

reason: MRA eligibility requires recent renal function and potassium data before recommendation.

clinical_sources.title: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA Guideline for the Management of Heart Failure
clinical_sources.note: Because MRA eligibility depends on renal function and potassium, missing values should block confident recommendation.

reason: RAAS-inhibiting therapy requires blood pressure and potassium review when data are missing.

clinical_sources.title: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA Guideline for the Management of Heart Failure
clinical_sources.note: RAAS-inhibiting therapy requires patient-specific review of blood pressure and potassium safety data.

clinical_sources.title: KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for CKD
clinical_sources.note: CKD guidance supports monitoring kidney function and potassium when using RAS-inhibiting treatment.

reason: Beta blocker initiation or titration requires heart rate review when data are missing.

clinical_sources.title: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA Guideline for the Management of Heart Failure
clinical_sources.note: Beta blocker use is guideline-directed in HFrEF, but missing heart rate prevents confident titration safety review.

reason: SGLT2 inhibitor eligibility requires renal function review when eGFR is missing.

clinical_sources.title: 2022 AHA/ACC/HFSA Guideline for the Management of Heart Failure
clinical_sources.note: SGLT2 inhibitor recommendation should be paired with renal function review before patient-specific use.

clinical_sources.title: 2023 ESC Focused Update of Heart Failure Guidelines
clinical_sources.note: The ESC update supports SGLT2 inhibitor use in heart failure, with drug-specific renal eligibility still needing review.
